user.title: Neo Smart Controller
user.description: Enter the controller's IP, its 24-character hub ID (from the Neo app: Menu → Controllers), and pick a protocol.

data.hub_id: Hub ID (24 characters from the Neo app)
data.motor_code: Motor code (optional, only for some hub models)

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the hub on that IP/port. Check the network and try again.
error.host_required: An IP address or hostname is required.
error.hub_id_invalid: Hub ID must be exactly 24 characters with no spaces.
error.protocol_invalid: Protocol must be HTTP or TCP.

data.hub_id: Hub ID (24 characters from the Neo app)

add_remote.title: Add a remote
add_remote.description: Enter the remote's ID1.ID2 prefix (e.g. {example}). Each remote registers itself with the hub by pressing its pairing button while the hub is in learning mode; the resulting prefix is shown in the Neo app's blind code (the part before the dash). Model is cosmetic — purely for your own bookkeeping.

remove_remote.title: Remove a remote
remove_remote.description: Removing a remote also removes all blinds attached to it.

add_blind.title: Add a blind
add_blind.description: Pick the remote, then enter the channel (01-15). Channel 15 broadcasts to every blind on the remote (room control).

data.name: Blind name
data.close_time: Close time (seconds, used for travel estimation)

error.host_required: An IP address or hostname is required.
error.hub_id_invalid: Hub ID must be exactly 24 characters with no spaces.
error.protocol_invalid: Protocol must be HTTP or TCP.
error.prefix_invalid: Use the format "ID1.ID2" with two numbers separated by a dot (e.g. 21.230).
error.prefix_exists: A remote with this prefix is already registered.
error.remote_invalid: Pick one of the registered remotes.
error.type_invalid: Pick one of: roller, venetian, tdbu.
error.blind_exists: A blind already exists on this remote+channel.

abort.no_blinds: There are no blinds to remove.
